channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Christopher Nolan’s The Odyssey boosts Melbourne IMAX cinema to record earnings
item.description: Christopher Nolan’s epic film 'The Odyssey' is driving Australia's box office to its highest level in seven years, with Melbourne's IMAX cinema reaping significant benefits. The film has grossed $18 m...

item.title: Astronomers Discover First Stellar-Mass Black Hole in Omega Centauri Cluster
item.description: Using over 20 years of Hubble Space Telescope data and recent James Webb Space Telescope observations, astronomers have identified the first stellar-mass black hole in the Omega Centauri cluster, a ma...

item.title: Mumbai model Rhiya Ahir calls for concrete action after PM Modi's assurance on NEET paper leak reforms
item.description: Model and actor Rhiya Ahir, who became widely recognised after standing in front of a Mumbai Police van during student protests over alleged examination paper leaks, has welcomed Prime Minister Narend...

item.title: NCAA Considers Suspension of Royal Air Maroc Due to Passenger Complaints
item.description: The Nigerian Civil Aviation Authority (NCAA) has warned Royal Air Maroc of possible suspension over repeated failures to address passenger complaints. Director Michael Achimugu accused the airline of ...

item.title: Aletta Rose faces psychiatric evaluation after courtroom disruptions
item.description: Aletta Rose, 63, accused of murdering her 79-year-old sister Constance Scholtz in March 2026, has become a focal point in the Bellville Magistrate’s Court due to her repeated disruptive behavior. The ...

item.title: Pakistan and Iran reaffirm commitment to Islamabad MoU amid regional tensions
item.description: Deputy Prime Minister Ishaq Dar emphasized the importance of fulfilling obligations under the Islamabad Memorandum of Understanding (MoU) during a meeting with Iranian Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi....

item.title: 23 Individuals Charged with Terror Meeting in Mwanza
item.description: The article discusses the legal proceedings against 23 individuals accused of organizing a terror-related meeting in Mwanza. The charges stem from allegations that the meeting aimed to coordinate acti...

item.title: Ruger Explains Reason Behind His Signature Eye Patch
item.description: Nigerian Afrobeats star Ruger has sparked conversation about his iconic eye patch during a livestream with comedian Shank Comics. The singer gave a playful new reason for always keeping one eye covere...

item.title: President Marcos faces declining approval amid legislative and foreign policy challenges
item.description: A Pulse Asia survey revealed that President Ferdinand Bongbong Marcos Jr.'s national approval and trust ratings fell by 7% points each, while disapproval and distrust rose by 5% and 7% respectively. H...

item.title: SAP Faces Challenges as AI Investments Raise Concerns About Cloud Transition
item.description: The Wall Street Journal article discusses growing concerns about SAP's ability to recover from its 'Saasocalypse' crisis, which refers to the challenges faced by the company during its transition to c...

item.title: Natalie Bassingthwaighte speaks about teenage relationship with an older married man and the pregnancy she later lost
item.description: Australian actress and singer Natalie Bassingthwaighte has spoken publicly about a traumatic relationship she experienced as a teenager during an appearance on the Inherited Podcast. Bassingthwaighte,...

item.title: Kano state launches initiative to create jobs for local pilots via new airline
item.description: Governor Abba Kabir Yusuf of Kano State has announced plans to establish a state-owned commercial airline as part of efforts to create employment opportunities for indigenous pilots. The governor reve...

item.title: Murang'a Governor Announces Run for 2027 Election on DCP Ticket
item.description: Murang'a Governor Irungu Kang'ata has declared his intention to contest the 2027 gubernatorial election on the Democracy for the Citizens Party (DCP) ticket, led by former Deputy President Rigathi Gac...

item.title: Alas Men Relegated to 5th Place in SEA V Cup After Loss to Indonesia
item.description: The Alas Pilipinas Men's volleyball team has been relegated to a 5th-place match in the SEA V Cup after suffering a defeat to Indonesia. This marks the second consecutive time the team has been placed...

item.title: Tanzania Insight News Update: July 24, 2026

item.title: PMD Issues Weather Advisory: Monsoon Rain and Thunderstorms Expected Across Pakistan from July 24-28
item.description: The Pakistan Meteorological Department (PMD) has issued a weather advisory predicting widespread monsoon rain and thunderstorms across the country from July 24 to 28. The forecast highlights heavy rai...

item.title: Ramaphosa's Legal Battle Over Alleged Stolen Cash Stalls As Court Halts Impeachment Probe
item.description: President Cyril Ramaphosa has successfully asked a South African court to pause impeachment proceedings against him over allegations of hiding $580,000 in stolen cash at his rural home. The Western Ca...

item.title: Marais Erasmus's Book Explores Cricket Umpiring and Life Beyond the Field
item.description: The article details the life and career of Marais Erasmus, a renowned cricket umpire, through his newly published book. Erasmus, known for his dynamic personality and rock-and-roll attitude, reflects ...

item.title: Lawyer Claims Southport Killer Attempting to Manipulate System via Psychiatric Transfer
item.description: Axel Rudakubana, 19, the convicted murderer of three children in Southport, has been transferred from HMP Belmarsh to Broadmoor psychiatric hospital. His lawyer, Chris Walker, alleges this move is an ...

item.title: Hospitality industry's gender imbalance highlighted at Brisbane food festival
item.description: The article discusses the gender imbalance in Brisbane's hospitality industry, highlighted by an all-male chef lineup at the James Street Food and Wine Trail festival. Despite the event's focus on sho...
